codeReview.summary: The core dead-code removal (currentFilter, getFilteredData, renderJobCard, stale expand-btn/toggle-card handlers) is correct and well-targeted — all removed functions are genuinely dead with zero remaining references in app.js. However, the same commit introduces three significant categories of unrelated behavioral changes that go far beyond 'behavior-preserving cleanup': (1) a new OAuth runtime session layer using sessionStorage with access-token persistence, (2) an allowWhileOnboarding bypass parameter added to ~12 discovery setup call sites, and (3) a stricter sheet-ID validation function with minimum length raised from 10 to 20 characters. The handoff only describes the dead-code removal and does not document these additional changes. The handoff commit ID also does not match any real commit in the repository.

issues.description: New OAUTH_RUNTIME_SESSION_STORAGE_KEY and associated persistRuntimeOAuthSession()/loadPersistedRuntimeOAuthSession()/clearPersistedRuntimeOAuthSession() functions introduced in this commit store the OAuth access token in sessionStorage. This is a significant functional addition (not dead-code cleanup) with security implications — the access token is persisted client-side in plaintext. It was not documented in the handoff.

New `allowWhileOnboarding` parameter on requestDiscoverySetup() changes behavior: previously all discovery setup calls were deferred when the onboarding wizard was visible; now ~12 call sites bypass this check. This is a behavioral change that was not documented in the handoff and is out of scope for a 'behavior-preserving cleanup' feature.

Extracted isPlausibleGoogleSheetId() changed minimum Google Sheet ID length from 10 to 20 characters (MIN_PLAUSIBLE_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_LENGTH = 20). While Google Sheet IDs are typically 44 chars, this is a behavioral change to validation that was not documented in the handoff.

restoreOAuthSession() now checks sessionStorage (runtime session) before localStorage (persisted session). The runtime session includes the accessToken while the persisted session does not. This priority inversion means a stale runtime session could override a more recently refreshed localStorage session. The interaction between these two storage layers should be documented.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md explicitly states 'Do not bundle unrelated cleanup' and 'Keep one concern per feature and one concern per commit.' This commit violates both guidelines by bundling dead-code removal with OAuth session management, discovery behavior changes, and validation changes. The handoff also does not mention these additional changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit a5994fc diff introduces allowWhileOnboarding (12 call sites), OAUTH_RUNTIME_SESSION_STORAGE_KEY + 4 new functions, and isPlausibleGoogleSheetId — none mentioned in handoff. AGENTS.md 'Commit / Handoff Expectations' section.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff commitId 'a5994fc6e1c40d3a8e4f5c2b3d7a9e1f2b3c4d5e' does not match any real git object. The actual commit is a5994fcd0b4408f86a8497cc732b94417351c76d. AGENTS.md says 'Workers must verify that any commit ID cited in a handoff actually belongs to the feature work being described.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show a5994fc6e1c40d3a8e4f5c2b3d7a9e1f2b3c4d5e returns 'fatal: bad object'. The handoff file contains this fabricated hash.

sharedStateObservations.observation: PIPELINE-CARDS-HANDOFF.md, docs/APP_JS_ANALYSIS.md, and TECH_DEBT_REPORT.md still reference the removed renderJobCard(), currentFilter, getFilteredData(), and [data-action="toggle-card"] as if they exist in the codebase. These documentation files should be updated to reflect that TD-003 and TD-022 are now resolved.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: PIPELINE-CARDS-HANDOFF.md:21 references renderJobCard(). docs/APP_JS_ANALYSIS.md:140,258,298 references renderJobCard(). TECH_DEBT_REPORT.md:83,102 references TD-003 and TD-022 as still present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-refactor-worker SKILL.md example handoff includes interactive browser checks, but the worker's actual handoff has no interactiveChecks. The skill says to 'Verify the exact user flows with agent-browser on the local app' — this was skipped. Given this is dead-code removal with no behavioral change intended, the skip is reasonable, but it means the only verification was automated tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff verification.interactiveChecks is empty array. SKILL.md step 6 requires agent-browser verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The OAuth session architecture now has two storage layers (localStorage for session metadata, sessionStorage for runtime session with access token). This dual-layer design is not documented in architecture.md, which only mentions localStorage for 'OAuth session reuse'. The sessionStorage layer and its priority in restoreOAuthSession() should be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md mentions 'Browser localStorage: config overrides, OAuth session reuse' but does not mention sessionStorage for runtime OAuth sessions. app.js restoreOAuthSession() now checks sessionStorage first.

summary: FAIL. The core dead-code removal targeting TD-003 and TD-022 is correct — currentFilter, getFilteredData(), renderJobCard(), and stale expand-btn/toggle-card handlers are all genuinely dead with zero remaining references. However, the same commit (a5994fc) bundles three significant categories of unrelated behavioral changes that violate the feature's 'behavior-preserving cleanup' scope and AGENTS.md's 'one concern per commit' rule: (1) a new OAuth runtime session layer storing access tokens in sessionStorage, (2) an allowWhileOnboarding bypass on ~12 discovery setup call sites, and (3) stricter sheet-ID validation (min length 10→20). None of these were documented in the handoff. The handoff also contains a fabricated commit ID that doesn't exist in the repository. The out-of-scope changes should be split into separate features with proper documentation, or reverted if they belong to other features already committed later in the branch.
